title: The Camp of Last Resort
summary:
Leaving Flagstaff, we wanted to stop by Sedona since we had bailed earlier in the year due to rainy weather. We did a nice little hike up Fay Canyon, which was relatively flat and filled with people. Though the trailhead was overflowing, we are optimistic we could still find dispersed camping, which we quickly realized was false. Sedona has become very strict about where you can camp for free and has designated big lots, which turn out to all be very full come April. After driving around for a couple of hours trying to find a spot, we bailed on Sedona entirely and tried to find the next closest free camping, which ended up being some BLM land on Beaverhead Flats road. The spot was meh but we were grateful to have arrived and spent the evening cozied in the van during one of our few nights of rain. In the morning, we did a quick mountain bike ride outside of Phoenix, but that ended up kind of being a fail too since most of the trails were blocked by construction fences. After that, we decided to high tail it to Tucson, where we knew family, a hot tub, and a nice bed were waiting for us.

-Helen
